title: Alnaschar
content:
آ آ آ آ Hereï؟½s yer toy balloons! All sizes!
آ آ آ آ Twenty cents for that. It rises
آ آ آ آ Jest as quick as that ï؟½ere, Miss,
آ آ آ آ Twice as big. Ye see it is
آ آ آ آ Some more fancy. Make it square
آ آ آ آ Fifty for ï؟½em both. Thatï؟½s fair.

آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s the sixth Iï؟½ve sold since noon.
آ آ آ آ Tradeï؟½s reviving. Just as soon
آ آ آ آ As this lotï؟½s worked off, Iï؟½ll take
آ آ آ آ Wholesale figgers. Make or break,
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s my motto! Then Iï؟½ll buy
آ آ آ آ In some first-class lottery
آ آ آ آ One half ticket, numbered right
آ آ آ آ As I dreamed about last night.

آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½ll fetch it. Donï؟½t tell me!
آ آ آ آ When a manï؟½s in luck, you see,
آ آ آ آ All things help him. Every chance
آ آ آ آ Hits him like an avalanche.
آ آ آ آ Hereï؟½s your toy balloons, Miss. Eh?
آ آ آ آ You wonï؟½t turn your face this way?
آ آ آ آ Mebbe youï؟½ll be glad some day.
آ آ آ آ With that clear ten thousand prize
آ آ آ آ This ï؟½yer trade Iï؟½ll drop, and rise
آ آ آ آ Into wholesale. No! Iï؟½ll take
آ آ آ آ Stocks in Wall Street. Make or break,
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s my motto! With my luck,
آ آ آ آ Whereï؟½s the chance of being stuck?
آ آ آ آ Call it sixty thousand, clear,
آ آ آ آ Made in Wall Street in one year.

آ آ آ آ Sixty thousand! Umph! Letï؟½s see!
آ آ آ آ Bond and mortgageï؟½ll do for me.
آ آ آ آ Good! That gal that passed me by
آ آ آ آ Scornful like why, mebbe I
آ آ آ آ Some dayï؟½ll hold in pawn why not?
آ آ آ آ All her fatherï؟½s prop. Sheï؟½ll spot
آ آ آ آ Whatï؟½s my little game, and see
آ آ آ آ What Iï؟½m afterï؟½s her. He! he!

آ آ آ آ He! he! When she comes to sue
آ آ آ آ Letï؟½s see! Whatï؟½s the thing to do?
آ آ آ آ Kick her? No! Thereï؟½s the perliss!
آ آ آ آ Sorter throw her off like this.
آ آ آ آ Hello! Stop! Help! Murder! Hey!
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s my whole stock got away,
آ آ آ آ Kiting on the house-tops! Lost!
آ آ آ آ All a poor manï؟½s fortin! Cost?
آ آ آ آ Twenty dollars! Eh! Whatï؟½s this?
آ آ آ آ Fifty cents! God bless ye, Miss!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bret Harte (Francis)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
